article.body_preview:
DHAKA, July 19, 2023 (BSS) - Six trade bodies have laid emphasis on continuing the 'Black Sea Grain Initiative (BSGI) Agreement' to avert human catastrophe.

They observed that the Bangladesh businesses are concerned about the disruption of smooth supply of wheat to feed millions of people around the world due to Russia's decision to axe the Black Sea Grain Initiative ("BSGI") from 17 July, said a press release today.

The bodies are Federation of Bangladesh Chambers of Commerce and Industry (FB

llm_connections.evidence_quote: disruption of smooth supply of wheat to feed millions of people around the world due to Russia's decision to axe the Black Sea Grain Initiative
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explicitly says the BSGI termination will disrupt the supply of wheat to countries worldwide, which directly corresponds to aggregate volumes of goods brought into countries (imports).

verification.quote_best_match: disruption of smooth supply of wheat to feed millions of people around the world due to Russia's decision to axe the Black Sea Grain Initiative
verification.judge_reasoning: The article contains the quoted language saying the disruption of wheat supply is 'due to Russia's decision to axe the Black Sea Grain Initiative' and explicitly states the discontinuation 'will ... cause' humanitarian and supply disruptions; it frames this impact globally (millions of people, exports to 45 countries), matching the imports-level scope.
